item.title: First time to the hospital
item.description: Our 2 year old son had been running a fever between 101 and 103 for 5 days. This is our first baby and he's been sick before but nothing like this. At day 5 I call the doctor because something isn't right.... Call it mothers intuition. I get a phone call appointment with the doc and tell them about the fever, he's not eating, barley drinking and he hasn't had a wet diaper in 24 hours. His lymph nodes under his jaw are very swollen and hurt when touched. They're also burning hot. My husband hears me listing off symptoms to the doctor and keeps saying, make sure you tell him About the heavy breathing and the snoring. The doc cuts me Off mid sentence while trying to explain he's breathing heavily and almost yells "TAKE HIM TO THE HOSPITAL". Doc explains 5 days is the limit for fevers like this in a 2 year old baby, and that I need to take him in now. I get off the phone and say, well we're going to the hospital. My husbands demeanor completely changes and he starts getting angry and giving me 1 word answers. I ask what's wrong and he looks at me like I'm giving him a speeding ticket... "well I guess I'm. Not hanging out with X and Y today... Haven't seen Y since highschool but whatever" I told him that his son needs him and I need him. This is the first time we've ever been in this situation and we need to go now. Doctors orders. So I got wake up our son and he's burning up again, he's had Advil 90 minutes ago but his fever has shot up and he's covered in sweat. My husband then starts yelling at me to just let him sleep. That he's not that sick and we can wait til Tuesday since it's the holiday weekend. I am shocked..... We go to the hospital and he leaves me there with our son and leaves. He goes to X's place and they leave town to go pick up Y in the city. He texts me. Twice to say they're leaving now and will be home in thirty. He gets back after 3 hours.... From a trip that was supposed to take 1 hour tops "I swear" We switch off at the hospital and he sees the doctor after 90 minutes. Son has an upper respiratory, throat and ear infection. Is given an Rx for a massive amount of antibiotics. They go home and I race to the city to get the script filled bc it's holiday Sunday and everything in our town and surrounding towns is closed. When I get back I tell him how not okay I am with how he handled this situation today. The then tells me that I treat him like I am His mother and he's leaving to go back to his friends house. I have no idea when he came home. But it must have been after 3am. This isn't the first time something like this has happened. When I was pregnant I had temporary kidney disease that would leave me doubled Over in pain(I would have a randon attack of horrible pain everytime my son would be sitting on my kidney) He got mad at me for it happening at our family Xmas party and blamed me for having to leave early... I don't know if I can do this anymore. We go to marriage counselling. I'm 7 years sober, have been treated for a 21 year battle with bulimia and am currently in EMDR Therapy for child sexual abuse I went through as a kid. I've done so much to improve myself and my relationships but he just keeps doing the same hurting thing. His pattern never changes. I can't believe I'm saying this, screaming into the void here... I'm not sure I can so this anymore...
item.dc:creator: YaHeardTurdFerguson

item.title: Americans - please help me understand
item.description: Why do American’s (speaking generally of course) find the idea of paying tax so repulsive? Last year our household made around $120,000 and paid roughly 25% ($30k) in tax. However, our family of 4 have been to our GP probably 10 times in that window and every visit has been bulk billed ($0 out of pocket), and my son had open heart surgery a few years back that required us to be out of town for a month and in total it cost us about $1200 which was all for excess accomodation as the government didn’t cover the entire amount. My son also goes to a special needs school and is in a class of 6 students with 3 teachers and we pay $0 a year in school fees. I thought America was super patriotic - why don’t you all want the best health and education systems in the world? Is it purely because you’d have to pay tax to get it? Help me out here…
item.dc:creator: BecauseIHaveWonAnAward
